topics.name: Daily Routine
questions: Describe your daily routine. What do you do from morning to evening?
What time do you usually wake up?
Do you like your daily routine? Why or why not?
Is there anything you want to change about your routine?

topics.name: Family
questions: Tell me about your family. How many people are there? What do they do?
Who do you live with?
What do you usually do with your family on weekends?
Who are you closest to in your family?

topics.name: Free Time
questions: Describe your free time activities. What do you usually do at weekends?
Do you prefer indoor or outdoor activities?
Have you tried any new activities recently?
Do you usually spend free time alone or with friends?

topics.name: Friends
questions: Tell me about your best friend. What is he or she like? What do you often do together?
How did you meet your best friend?
What qualities do you value in a friend?
Do you prefer having many friends or a few close ones?

topics.name: Hometown
questions: Tell me about your hometown. Where is it? What do you like about it?
What is the weather like in your hometown?
Is your hometown a good place for young people? Why?
Would you like to live there in the future?

topics.name: Daily Routine
questions: Describe your daily routine on a weekday. What do you usually do in the morning, afternoon, and evening? Why is this routine suitable for you?
What time do you usually wake up?
Do you like your daily routine? Why or why not?
Is there anything you want to change about your routine?

topics.name: Favorite Food
questions: Talk about your favorite food. What is it? How often do you eat it? Why do you like it?
Can you cook this food yourself?
Have you ever tried cooking a new dish? How was it?
Do you think eating habits in your country are changing?

topics.name: Favorite Hobby
questions: Talk about your favorite hobby or activity. How did you start it? Why do you enjoy it?
How much time do you spend on this hobby each week?
Have you ever taught someone else this hobby?
Would you like to turn this hobby into a career?

topics.name: Memorable Trip
questions: Describe a memorable trip you have taken. Where did you go? What did you do? Why was it memorable?
Who did you travel with?
What was the most surprising thing about that trip?
Would you recommend this destination to others?

topics.name: Useful Mobile App
questions: Talk about a mobile app you often use. What is it used for? How does it help you in your daily life? Why do you think it is useful?
How did you discover this app?
Are there any disadvantages of using it?
Do you think people rely too much on apps nowadays?

content.situation: Look at the two pictures. Compare and contrast life in a big city and life in the countryside. Which would you prefer to live in and why?
options: Move to a big city for better career opportunities
Stay in the countryside for a peaceful lifestyle
Try living in both places at different stages of life

topic: Online Shopping vs Shopping in Stores

content.situation: Compare shopping online and shopping in stores. What are the advantages and disadvantages of each?
options: Shop mostly online for convenience and lower prices
Shop in stores to see and try products before buying
Combine both methods depending on the type of product

content.situation: Compare reading printed books and reading e-books. Which do you think is better for students and why?
options: Switch completely to e-books for portability
Continue reading printed books for a better experience
Use both formats depending on the situation

content.situation: Compare studying alone and studying in a group. What are the advantages and disadvantages of each?
options: Study alone to focus better and avoid distractions
Study in groups to share ideas and stay motivated
Alternate between both methods depending on the subject

topic: Travelling by Car vs Travelling by Bus

content.situation: Look at the two pictures. Compare travelling by car and travelling by bus. Which would you choose for a long trip and why?
options: Travel by car for comfort and flexibility
Take the bus to save money and reduce pollution
Choose based on the distance and purpose of the trip

topic: Should Teenagers Use Smartphones at School?

content.centralIdea: Some people think teenagers should be allowed to use smartphones at school, while others think phones should be banned during class time. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestions: Think about learning benefits and classroom distractions
Consider communication with parents and safety
Discuss whether rules should depend on the lesson
content.followUpQuestion: What rules about smartphone use at school would you introduce if you were a principal?

topic: A Skill You Want to Improve

topics.name: A Skill You Want to Improve
questions: Describe a skill you want to improve in the future. Why is it important to you, and how are you planning to improve it?
What makes this skill difficult to learn?
Who could help you improve it?
How would your life change if you became very good at this skill?

content.situation: Compare the roles of traditional media and social media in modern society. How do they influence public opinion differently?
options: Rely mainly on traditional media for verified information
Use social media as the primary news source for speed
Cross-check both sources before forming opinions

content.situation: Compare online learning and face-to-face learning for university students. How do they affect students’ motivation and learning outcomes differently?
options: Make online learning the default for universities
Keep face-to-face learning as the primary method
Adopt a hybrid model combining both approaches

content.situation: Compare living in the city and living in the countryside. How do these two environments influence young people’s lifestyle and future opportunities differently?
options: Encourage young people to move to cities for career growth
Develop rural areas to make countryside living more attractive
Let individuals decide based on personal priorities

content.situation: Compare doing a part-time job and focusing only on study while at university. How can students choose the more suitable option for their own situation?
options: Focus entirely on studies to achieve the best academic results
Take a part-time job to gain practical experience and income
Balance both by choosing a job related to your field of study

content.situation: Compare public transport and private vehicles in big cities. Which is a better solution for daily travel, and why?
options: Invest heavily in public transport infrastructure
Encourage private vehicle use with better roads
Implement congestion pricing to reduce traffic

content.centralIdea: Should university education be free for all students? Present arguments for both sides and give your opinion.
suggestions: Consider the economic burden on governments
Think about quality vs accessibility
Look at examples from countries with free education
content.followUpQuestion: How would you fund free university education if you were in charge?

content.centralIdea: How can online learning be made more effective for university students? Give reasons and examples.
suggestions: Consider the role of technology and internet access
Think about student motivation and self-discipline
Discuss teacher training for online platforms
content.followUpQuestion: What specific technology do you think could improve online learning the most?

content.centralIdea: Some people think that university students should have part-time jobs, while others believe they should focus only on studying. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestions: Think about time management challenges
Consider financial independence vs academic performance
Discuss the type of jobs that benefit students most
content.followUpQuestion: Should universities limit the number of hours students can work?

content.centralIdea: Do you think public transport should be improved to encourage more people to use it? Give reasons and examples.
suggestions: Consider environmental benefits
Think about cost and accessibility for different income groups
Discuss examples of successful public transport systems
content.followUpQuestion: What is the biggest barrier to improving public transport in your city?

content.centralIdea: Some people say that young people today have too much freedom. Do you agree or disagree? Give reasons and examples.
suggestions: Compare freedom today with previous generations
Consider the influence of technology and social media
Think about the relationship between freedom and responsibility
content.followUpQuestion: How can parents balance giving freedom while ensuring their children make good decisions?

content.centralIdea: Analyze the impact of artificial intelligence on higher education. Consider its effects on teaching methods, student learning, and academic integrity.
suggestions: Examine AI-powered personalized learning
Consider risks to academic integrity (e.g. AI-generated essays)
Discuss the changing role of teachers
content.followUpQuestion: Do you think AI will eventually replace human teachers in some subjects?

content.centralIdea: Propose a strategy for universities to prepare students for the skills required in the future workplace. Explain which abilities should be prioritized and how they can be developed effectively.
suggestions: Identify skills most likely to be automated
Discuss the importance of soft skills vs technical skills
Consider how universities can partner with industry
content.followUpQuestion: Which single skill do you believe will be most valuable in 10 years?

content.centralIdea: Discuss the ethical implications of genetic engineering in humans. Consider the scientific, social, and moral dimensions of this issue.
suggestions: Distinguish between therapeutic and enhancement applications
Consider issues of consent and inequality
Discuss existing regulations and their adequacy
content.followUpQuestion: Where would you draw the line between acceptable and unacceptable genetic modifications?

content.centralIdea: Evaluate the role of international organizations in addressing global inequality. How effective have they been, and what changes would you recommend?
suggestions: Evaluate the track record of organizations like the UN and World Bank
Consider structural causes of inequality
Discuss whether aid creates dependency
content.followUpQuestion: Is it more effective to address inequality through economic growth or redistribution?

content.centralIdea: Evaluate the effectiveness of sustainable urban development strategies in improving quality of life in large cities. What are the main challenges, and which solutions seem most practical?
suggestions: Discuss green infrastructure and renewable energy
Consider affordable housing challenges
Examine examples of sustainable cities
content.followUpQuestion: Can developing countries afford to prioritize sustainability over rapid growth?
